root_cause_summary: ff_vmafmotion_init() in libavfilter/vf_vmafmotion.c stores caller-supplied width (w) and height (h) into s->width / s->height and allocates a w×h buffer without validating minimum dimensions. Downstream convolution_y_10bit/convolution_y_8bit and convolution_x (called via ff_vmafmotion_process) use a filter of width 5 (radius=2). When h<3, the bottom-border loop iterates at negative i (e.g. i=-1), computing dst[-1*dst_stride + j] OOB write; the mirror-reflection expression h-(i_tap-h+1) also produces negative i_tap for src reads. When w<3, analogous OOB access occurs in convolution_x via negative j_tap. The vulnerability is exploitable by providing a frame with width or height smaller than 3 pixels to the vmafmotion filter.
fix_summary: Add early-return guard if (w < 3 || h < 3) return AVERROR(EINVAL) at the top of ff_vmafmotion_init, before any dimension-dependent allocation or assignment, rejecting undersized inputs. The original fix is commit 82ad1b76751b (2021-05-29); the other 5 commits are cherry-pick backports to release branches.

root_cause_summary: In aac_encode_frame(), the lambda adjustment loop uses FFMIN(s->lambda * ratio, 65536.f) which only caps the upper bound. After repeated sqrtf(ratio) iterations, ratio can become extremely small, causing s->lambda * ratio to underflow to 0. Since s->lambda is used as a divisor in multiple downstream code paths in aaccoder.c (lambda / threshold, 100.0f/lambda, lambda / 120.f) and aacenc.c (s->lambda / global_quality), a zero-valued s->lambda produces a division-by-zero crash (CWE-369).
fix_summary: Replace one-sided clamp FFMIN(s->lambda * ratio, 65536.f) with two-sided clamp av_clipf(s->lambda * ratio, FLT_MIN, 65536.f), enforcing a minimum value of FLT_MIN on s->lambda to prevent underflow to zero. Add #include <float.h> for FLT_MIN. All 7 fix commits are semantically identical backports across branches.

root_cause_summary: In ff_frame_thread_encoder_init(), AVCodecContext struct for each worker thread is allocated via avcodec_alloc_context3() inside the loop body with loop-local scope. If any subsequent per-thread initialization step fails (e.g., avcodec_open2(), pthread_create(), av_opt_copy()), goto fail is taken. The fail label has no avcodec_close()/av_freep() for the thread_avctx allocated in the failing iteration. Because thread_avctx is scoped to the loop body, the pointer is lost, and the allocated AVCodecContext is never freed — a CWE-401 memory leak. Additionally, in the variant using av_opt_copy(), the struct-copy *thread_avctx = *avctx copies the parent's internal/hw_frames_ctx pointers before av_opt_copy() is called; if av_opt_copy() fails and the code later tries to clean up, the copied dangling pointers could cause use-after-free or double-free in avcodec_close().
fix_summary: Two structural changes: (1) Hoist AVCodecContext *thread_avctx declaration from loop-local to function scope initialized to NULL, enabling the fail-path to reference the current iteration's allocation. (2) Add avcodec_close(thread_avctx) and av_freep(&thread_avctx) at the fail label to properly release the orphaned context. In the av_opt_copy() variant (upstream/master commit 7bba0dd6382e), also move thread_avctx->priv_data=tmpv, thread_avctx->internal=NULL, and add thread_avctx->hw_frames_ctx=NULL before av_opt_copy(), so that if av_opt_copy() fails, avcodec_close() on the fail path does not operate on parent-dangling internal/hw_frames_ctx pointers.

root_cause_summary: In RestoreMSCWarning() at coders/pdf.c, GetPixelIndex() returns Quantum (double in HDRI builds; wider unsigned integer in Q16 builds). The return value is cast directly to unsigned char without an intermediate signed integer conversion. When Quantum is double and the pixel index value is outside [0,255], the direct double-to-unsigned-char cast is undefined behavior (CWE-190 Integer Overflow). All four call sites (lines 1992, 2034, 2509, 2546 in the vulnerable parent a9d563d3d7) exhibit this pattern.
fix_summary: Commit 872ffe6d0131 (19:38 UTC, parent a9d563d3d7) fixed the Ascii85Encode+tile_image call site (line 2546) by wrapping GetPixelIndex() with (ssize_t). Commit 9dd1c7e1f8f6 (19:43 UTC, parent=872ffe6d0131) fixed the remaining three call sites: *q++= with image (line 1992), Ascii85Encode with image (line 2034), and *q++= with tile_image (line 2509), all with the same (unsigned char) ((ssize_t) GetPixelIndex(...)) pattern. The fix converts Quantum through ssize_t before narrowing to unsigned char, making the double-to-integer conversion well-defined.

root_cause_summary: In DrawPrimitive(), SetImageInfo(clone_info,0,exception) is called with frames=0, which causes SetImageInfo to open the referenced file to read magic bytes for format detection. An attacker can craft an SVG image element referencing a path that triggers a denial of service (e.g., slow devices, infinite recursion, resource exhaustion) during this unnecessary file open. The opened filename is immediately overwritten afterward, making the I/O entirely wasted.
fix_summary: Changed SetImageInfo(clone_info,0,exception) to SetImageInfo(clone_info,1,exception) at MagickCore/draw.c line 5538. With frames=1, SetImageInfo skips the magic-byte file-sniffing branch (if (frames==0) { AcquireImage; OpenBlob; ReadBlob; CloseBlob; } ), relying on filename/extension-based format detection instead of opening the referenced file.

root_cause_summary: RegisterStaticModules() iterates all statically compiled coders and registers them without any authorization check against the ModulePolicyDomain from policy.xml. A policy entry like <policy domain="module" rights="none" pattern="PS"/> is silently bypassed because the bulk registration path never calls IsRightsAuthorized. Additionally, RegisterStaticModule() uses only ReadPolicyRights (missing WritePolicyRights), and OpenModule() uses AllPolicyRights (overly broad, incoherent with the policy model).
fix_summary: Commit 1 (35893e7cad78) adds a missing IsRightsAuthorized(ModulePolicyDomain, AllPolicyRights, ...) guard to RegisterStaticModules() but uses AllPolicyRights which is too strict. Commit 2 (01faddbe2711) corrects all three sites to use ReadPolicyRights|WritePolicyRights: OpenModule() in module.c changes from AllPolicyRights, RegisterStaticModule() in static.c changes from ReadPolicyRights, and RegisterStaticModules() in static.c changes from AllPolicyRights to the local variable rights initialized to ReadPolicyRights|WritePolicyRights.

source_refs.snippet: MagickExport void RegisterStaticModules(void) { size_t extent; ssize_t i; extent=sizeof(MagickModules)/sizeof(MagickModules[0]); for (i=0; i < (ssize_t) extent; i++) { if (MagickModules[i].registered == MagickFalse) { (void) (MagickModules[i].register_module)(); MagickModules[i].registered=MagickTrue; } } }
Added missing policy checks in RegisterStaticModules.

root_cause_summary: In curl_wssl_init_ctx() (lib/vquic/vquic-tls.c, wolfSSL QUIC TLS init), 4 error-handling blocks call failf()+goto out; without setting the result variable to an error code. When ctx_setup() succeeds and sets result=CURLE_OK, a subsequent wolfSSL failure (cipher list, curves, keylog, or CA cert) jumps to out: with result still CURLE_OK, causing the function to return success. The caller proceeds with QUIC connection using an incompletely initialized SSL context, silently bypassing certificate verification and TLS configuration.
fix_summary: Added result = CURLE_BAD_FUNCTION_ARGUMENT before goto out; in cipher-list and curves error paths; result = CURLE_NOT_BUILT_IN before goto out; in keylog-callback-not-built error path; result = CURLE_SSL_CACERT before goto out; in CA-cert-verify-location error path. All 4 hunks ensure error codes are propagated through the out: label instead of returning stale CURLE_OK.

root_cause_summary: In utf8asn1str() at lib/vtls/x509asn1.c:396 (parent 185a05e9), free(buf) is called on buf which is a stack-allocated local array (char buf[4] at line 377). Calling free() on a non-heap pointer is undefined behavior: most allocators detect this and abort (DoS), but some may corrupt heap metadata leading to memory corruption. The bug is reached when parsing a crafted ASN.1 UTF-8 string containing a Unicode codepoint >= 0x00200000, triggering the error-handling path.

root_cause_summary: Curl's metalink download feature downloads content from a URL listed in the metalink XML, then verifies the downloaded file against an expected hash from the same XML. When the hash verification fails (indicating the content was tampered with or came from a compromised mirror), curl only prints a text warning 'FAILED (digest mismatch)' to stderr and sets a dead flag `metalink_next_res = 1` that no transfer-scheduling code ever consumes. The corrupted/tampered file is NOT deleted from disk, and the transfer returns CURLE_OK to the caller, so the integrity failure is invisible to the program exit code. An explicit TODO comment at tool_operate.c:608-609 (pre-fix) reads 'We want to try next resource when download was not successful. How to know that?' confirming the retry-from-alternative-URL mechanism was never implemented.
fix_summary: The entire metalink feature is removed. Two files (src/tool_metalink.c, src/tool_metalink.h) are deleted. The `--metalink` CLI option is replaced with `errorf(global, "--metalink is disabled\n"); return PARAM_BAD_USE;`. All metalink configuration fields (`use_metalink`, `metalinkfile_list` in struct OperationConfig) and per-transfer fields (`metalink`, `metalink_next_res`, `mlfile`, `mlres` in struct per_transfer) are deleted. All metalink-related code paths in src/tool_operate.c, src/tool_help.c, src/tool_getparam.c, src/tool_cfgable.h, src/tool_operate.h, src/tool_sdecls.h are removed or gated out. The fix is a feature removal rather than repairing the broken hash-mismatch handling.

root_cause_summary: In remoteip_modify_request(), the root trusted address temp_sa was initialized from c->client_addr (the raw TCP connection address). When an internal redirect/subrequest occurs (e.g., ErrorDocument 40x), the handler runs again with the same c->client_addr. If the proxy IP is in the trusted list, the second invocation re-parses the X-Forwarded-For chain from the original TCP address, allowing a spoofed IP from the header to overwrite r->useragent_addr. This is an input validation invariant failure: during the second invocation, the code should use the already-parsed r->useragent_addr rather than re-deriving from c->client_addr.
fix_summary: Changed temp_sa initialization from 'c->client_addr' to 'r->useragent_addr ? r->useragent_addr : c->client_addr'. If r->useragent_addr was already populated by a prior invocation (e.g., the original request before internal redirect), it is used as the root trusted address instead of the raw connection address, preventing re-derivation of the useragent IP during subrequests.

source_refs.snippet: mod_remoteip: Use r->useragent_addr as the root trusted address for verifying. This fixes issue resulting in setting of bad useragent_ip when internal redirection has been generated as response to the request (typically as result of "ErrorDocument 40x").

root_cause_summary: The vulnerability has two independent root causes. Primary: the Cache-Digest header parsing code (h2_push_diary_digest_set / h2_push_diary_digest64_set) accepts attacker-controlled Golomb-coded set data that, when processed during subsequent HTTP/2 PUSH operations, can cause out-of-bounds memory access via underflow of diary->entries->nelts in move_to_last (lastidx = nelts-1 when nelts==0) or uncontrolled array growth in h2_push_diary_append. Secondary: h2_stream_add_header unconditionally increments request_headers_added before validation, add_trailer and h2_req_add_header lack merged-field size checks against limit_req_fieldsize, and LimitRequestFields 0 is not treated as 'disabled'.
fix_summary: Commit a61223e9cb90 removes the entire Cache-Digest parsing infrastructure (h2_push_diary_digest_set, h2_push_diary_digest64_set, gset_decoder, and related gset_decode functions), eliminates the Cache-Digest header processing from h2_push_collect_update, adds bounds guards to move_to_last (if nelts<=0 return), replaces append-or-replace logic with remove_first+append pattern using a while(nelts>=N) loop, and changes move_to_last return type from pointer to void. Commit 971fc8f5b5d6 restructures h2_stream_add_header with goto cleanup error handling, introduces a was_added flag to count only genuinely new headers, adds max_field_len checks to add_trailer and h2_req_add_header for merged-field size enforcement, fixes LimitRequestFields 0 to disable the limit, and changes set_h1_header to always return 1.

uncertainty: The exact crash mechanism in the Cache-Digest parser depends on apr_array internals and heap state; the underflow in move_to_last (lastidx = nelts-1 when nelts==0) produces a memmove with SIZE_MAX length which is reliably a crash, but other code paths through gset_decode_next may have different failure modes (OOB read vs infinite loop).

The relationship between the two fix commits is purely additive (two independent bugs in the same component), not sequential (one fix does not depend on the other). Any tag that has both old-pattern bugs is vulnerable.

root_cause_summary: mod_http2 used the master HTTP/2 session connection (m->c) as the logging target for TRACE/DEBUG-level log statements inside functions invoked from secondary (worker thread) connections. Because secondary connections execute stream processing in separate threads, concurrent ap_log_cerror() calls on the master connection's log/apr_pool_t structures caused undefined behavior through unsynchronized pool access. The protocol invariant violated is that per-stream/thread operations must use thread-local connection records (stream->task->c) for all side-effectful operations including logging. This was exploitable only when LogLevel for mod_http2 was set to trace/debug levels.
fix_summary: Commit 63a0a87efa09 performs a major refactoring that renames 'slave' to 'secondary' terminology and, critically, redirects all TRACE/DEBUG logging inside secondary-thread-executed functions (out_open, task_done, out_close, mplx_be_happy, stream_destroy_iter, etc.) from the master connection m->c to the secondary connection task->c or stream->task->c. It also adds a naming convention (m_/s_/t_/mst_) to statically encode which thread context each function belongs to. Commit 971fc8f5b5d6 fixes a separate but related logic bug where stream->request_headers_added was unconditionally incremented before validation, and repeated headers with the same name were over-counted against the LimitRequestFields limit; it introduces a was_added output parameter and proper goto cleanup flow.

Changed slave->keepalives to secondary->keepalives, m->spare_slaves to m->spare_secondary, h2_slave_destroy to h2_secondary_destroy. The critical fix is redirecting h2_task_logio_add_bytes_out from slave to secondary and h2_beam_log from m->c to secondary.
Changed spare_slaves to spare_secondary, h2_slave_create to h2_secondary_create, slave to secondary. The secondary connection is now created from the secondary thread context.
Changed ap_log_cerror(..., m->c, ...) to ap_log_cerror(..., task->c, ...) and h2_beam_log(..., m->c, ...) to h2_beam_log(..., task->c, ...). Fixes logging-on-wrong-connection in out_close.

Removed unconditional ++stream->request_headers_added. Added was_added tracking, limit_req_fields > 0 guard, proper goto cleanup. Fixes LimitRequestFields 0 disabling and repeated-header over-counting.

Changed multiple ap_log_cerror(..., m->c, ...) to ap_log_cerror(..., task->c, ...) inside s_task_done. Also changed log level of redo message from APLOG_INFO to APLOG_DEBUG to reduce log noise on secondary connections.
Renamed slave_id to sec_id, updated all log messages from 'slave' to 'secondary', changed pool tag from h2_slave_conn to h2_secondary_conn, updated comments from slave to secondary.
Changed filter names from H2_SLAVE_IN/H2_SLAVE_OUT to H2_SECONDARY_IN/H2_SECONDARY_OUT. Updated log message from 'h2_slave' to 'h2_secondary'.

FixEvidence.semantic_change: Commit 63a0a87efa09 refactors the mod_http2 threading model from implicit thread-context (where functions could be called from either master or secondary thread with no annotation) to an explicit naming convention that statically encodes the allowed calling thread. The critical semantic change is that all logging operations that run on behalf of a secondary connection use that secondary connection's conn_rec (task->c), not the master's (m->c), preventing concurrent pool access. Commit 971fc8f5b5d6 changes the header counting semantics from 'count every header attempt' to 'count only headers that are actually newly inserted', and from 'always enforce limit_req_fields even when 0' to 'limit_req_fields 0 disables the limit'.

preconditions: LogLevel for mod_http2 must be at TRACE1/TRACE2/TRACE3 or DEBUG for the vulnerable code paths to be reachable (ap_log_cerror with APLOG_TRACE1/2/3 and APLOG_DEBUG only emit when log level is at least that threshold)

A secondary connection must be active and executing stream processing in a separate thread (the m_stream_destroy_iter, s_task_done, t_out_open, s_out_close, s_mplx_be_happy functions only execute on secondary threads)
The APR allocator for the master connection's pool must be shared between threads without per-call synchronization on pool-tagging operations (apr_pool_tag, ap_log_cerror internal pool access)
configuration_guards: Setting LogLevel for mod_http2 to 'info' or higher (above 'debug') disables all APLOG_TRACE and APLOG_DEBUG level log statements, preventing the vulnerable code path from executing

The LimitRequestFields directive: setting it to 0 disables the limit entirely (after fix); before fix, setting it to 0 still enforced the limit because the guard if (session->s->limit_req_fields > 0) was missing

Not vulnerable when mod_http2 is not loaded or not used
Not vulnerable when using a non-threaded MPM (e.g., prefork) since no concurrent thread access occurs

root_cause_summary: unix_gc() in net/unix/garbage.c unconditionally purges all skbs in detected socket cycles (__skb_queue_purge(&hitlist)), including skbs created by io_uring's __io_scm_file_account(). These io_uring skbs have a different lifecycle: their file references are managed by io_uring's registered file table, not by Unix socket refcounts. When unix_gc() frees them, the underlying files are released while io_uring still holds active pointers to them, causing a use-after-free when io_uring later accesses its registered file table.
fix_summary: Add a scm_io_uring marker bit to struct sk_buff, set it in __io_scm_file_account(), and add guard sequences in unix_gc() that detect this marker to exempt io_uring skbs from the GC purge cycle: (1) before __skb_queue_purge(&hitlist), walk the hitlist and return scm_io_uring skbs to their socket's receive queue; (2) after the purge, move io_uring-related gc_candidates back to gc_inflight_list instead of letting them be destroyed.

root_cause_summary: Race condition (CWE-362) in drivers/char/pcmcia/scr24x_cs.c between scr24x_open() and scr24x_remove(). scr24x_open() calls kref_get(&dev->refcnt) and assigns filp->private_data = dev without holding dev->lock and without checking dev->dev validity indicator. Concurrently scr24x_remove() sets dev->dev = NULL then calls kref_put, which can trigger scr24x_delete() → kfree(dev). After remove completes, any read/write on a fd that raced through open() accesses filp->private_data pointing to freed memory, producing use-after-free. read() and write() check if (!dev->dev) but this check itself dereferences the freed dev pointer when the device is freed.
fix_summary: Entire PCMCIA char driver subsystem removed: scr24x_cs.c, cm4000_cs.c, cm4040_cs.c, synclink_cs.c, plus their headers (include/linux/cm4000_cs.h, include/uapi/linux/cm4000_cs.h, cm4040_cs.h), Kconfig/Makefile, MAINTAINERS entries, documentation references, and defconfig entries. This is a 'vulnerable_branch_removed' archetype fix—the entire vulnerable code path is deleted rather than repaired with synchronization guards.

source_refs.snippet: static int scr24x_open(struct inode *inode, struct file *filp) { struct scr24x_dev *dev = container_of(inode->i_cdev, struct scr24x_dev, c_dev); kref_get(&dev->refcnt); filp->private_data = dev; return stream_open(inode, filp); }

static void scr24x_remove(struct pcmcia_device *link) { struct scr24x_dev *dev = (struct scr24x_dev *)link->priv; device_destroy(scr24x_class, MKDEV(MAJOR(scr24x_devt), dev->devno)); cdev_del(&dev->c_dev); clear_bit(dev->devno, scr24x_minors); mutex_lock(&dev->lock); dev->dev = NULL; mutex_unlock(&dev->lock); kref_put(&dev->refcnt, scr24x_delete); }
char: pcmcia: remove all the drivers. These char PCMCIA drivers are buggy[1] and receive only minimal care. It was concluded[2], that we should try to remove most pcmcia drivers completely.

static int scr24x_release(struct inode *inode, struct file *filp) { struct scr24x_dev *dev = filp->private_data; /* We must not take the dev->lock here as scr24x_delete() might be called to remove the dev structure altogether. */ kref_put(&dev->refcnt, scr24x_delete); return 0; }
removed_critical_sequence includes: kref_get(&dev->refcnt); kref_put(&dev->refcnt, scr24x_delete); if (!dev->dev); mutex_lock_interruptible(&dev->lock)

root_cause_summary: In fs/ksmbd/smb2misc.c:smb2_get_data_area_len(), an early-return block that checked hdr->Status != 0 as an 'error response' indicator caused the function to skip all data-area offset/length validation for SMB2_TREE_CONNECT requests with a non-zero Status field. Because the function returned ret=0 with *off=0, *len=0, the caller smb2_calc_size treated the packet as valid with no data area, bypassing the buffer size check. The smb2_tree_connect handler then read req->PathLength directly from the request buffer and passed it to smb_strndup_from_utf16(), causing an out-of-bounds read when PathLength exceeds the actual buffer.

root_cause_summary: In opj_j2k_update_image_dimensions(), p_image->x0/y0/x1/y1 (OPJ_UINT32 parsed from image header) are cast to OPJ_INT32 and passed to opj_int_ceildiv() without a range check. These corrupted dimensions propagate through tcd.c buffer calculations to opj_t1_clbl_decode_processor(), which reads/writes outside allocated heap buffers (CWE-787).

root_cause_summary: In pngtoimage() at src/bin/jp2/convertpng.c:225-228, image->x1 and image->y1 are computed by adding image->x0/image->y0 twice: once as the leading term (image->x0 + ...) and once redundantly at the end (... + 1 + image->x0). When the -d option sets non-zero image_offset_x0/image_offset_y0, x1/y1 are inflated by an extra offset term, causing the JPEG 2000 encoder downstream to compute buffer bounds that exceed the actual allocation (allocated as width*height per component via opj_image_create). This enables a heap-buffer overflow (CWE-787) when the encoder writes into component data planes using the inflated x1/y1 region.
fix_summary: Removed the redundant + image->x0 and + image->y0 from the x1/y1 computation so each offset is counted exactly once: x1 = x0 + (width-1)*subsampling_dx + 1; y1 = y0 + (height-1)*subsampling_dy + 1.

source_refs.snippet: pngtoimage(): fix wrong computation of x1,y1 if -d option is used, that would result in a heap buffer overflow (fixes #1284)

image->x1 = (OPJ_UINT32)(image->x0 + (width  - 1) * (OPJ_UINT32) params->subsampling_dx + 1 + image->x0);
image->y1 = (OPJ_UINT32)(image->y0 + (height - 1) * (OPJ_UINT32) params->subsampling_dy + 1 + image->y0);

necessary_conditions: params->image_offset_x0 > 0 (non-zero x-offset via -d option), causing image->x0 > 0
params->image_offset_y0 > 0 (non-zero y-offset via -d option), causing image->y0 > 0
image->x1 computation erroneously adds image->x0 twice: x0 + (w-1)*dx + 1 + x0 instead of x0 + (w-1)*dx + 1
image->y1 computation erroneously adds image->y0 twice: y0 + (h-1)*dy + 1 + y0 instead of y0 + (h-1)*dy + 1
Downstream encoder code uses inflated x1/y1 values for buffer sizing or write bounds, exceeding the component data allocation (width*height per opj_image_create)

image->y1 = .*\+ image->y0\)
subsampling_dx \+ 1 \+ image->x0
subsampling_dy \+ 1 \+ image->y0

Any version where this file does not exist or the pngtoimage function is not compiled is NOT_AFFECTED

root_cause_summary: In opj_dwt_calc_explicit_stepsizes(), level = tccp->numresolutions - 1 - resno is used as a column index into opj_dwt_norms_real[4][10] without bounds checking. When tccp->numresolutions > 11 (orient==0) or > 10 (orient>0), level exceeds the array's 10-column limit, causing a global buffer out-of-bounds read.

root_cause_summary: ossl_ffc_validate_public_key_partial() 和 ossl_ffc_validate_private_key() 在访问函数参数之前缺少 NULL 检查。在 ossl_ffc_validate_public_key_partial() 中，当 params、pub_key 或 params->p 为 NULL 时，BN_copy(tmp, params->p) 会发生 NULL 指针解引用。在 ossl_ffc_validate_private_key() 中，未检查的 priv 或 upper 被传递给 BN_cmp()。攻击者可以通过向 EVP_PKEY_public_check() 提供格式错误的 DSA 公钥来触发此漏洞，从而导致拒绝服务（应用程序崩溃）。
fix_summary: 在 ossl_ffc_validate_public_key_partial() 入口处添加了 NULL 守卫：if (params == NULL || pub_key == NULL || params->p == NULL)，在 ossl_ffc_validate_private_key() 入口处添加了 NULL 守卫：if (priv == NULL || upper == NULL)。两种守卫均返回新的错误码 FFC_ERROR_PASSED_NULL_PARAM (0x40)，该错误码也已在 include/internal/ffc.h 中定义。同时添加了覆盖率测试。

source_refs.snippet: if (params == NULL || pub_key == NULL || params->p == NULL) { *ret = FFC_ERROR_PASSED_NULL_PARAM; return 0; }

source_refs.function_context: int ossl_ffc_validate_private_key(const BIGNUM *upper, const BIGNUM *priv, int *ret)
source_refs.snippet: if (priv == NULL || upper == NULL) { *ret = FFC_ERROR_PASSED_NULL_PARAM; goto err; }
Fix NULL deference when validating FFC public key. When attempting to do a BN_Copy of params->p there was no NULL check. Since BN_copy does not check for NULL this is a NULL reference.
*ret = 0; ctx = BN_CTX_new_ex(NULL); if (ctx == NULL) goto err;

source_refs.function_context: int ossl_ffc_validate_private_key(const BIGNUM *upper, const BIGNUM *priv, int *ret)

执行路径到达 ossl_ffc_validate_public_key_partial()，其中 params 指针在未进行 NULL 检查的情况下被解引用
或者执行路径到达 ossl_ffc_validate_private_key()，其中 priv 或 upper 在未进行 NULL 检查的情况下被传递给 BN_cmp()
vulnerable_code_patterns: 缺少前导 NULL 守卫的指针解引用 / 指针参数使用
ossl_ffc_validate_public_key_partial() 入口：立即进入 BN_CTX_new_ex() 和相关指针算术操作，而未验证 params 或 pub_key 是否为 NULL
ossl_ffc_validate_private_key() 入口：立即进入 BN_cmp(priv, BN_value_one())，而未验证 priv 或 upper 是否为 NULL
negative_evidence: 无：漏洞仅存在于 ossl_ffc_validate_public_key_partial() 和 ossl_ffc_validate_private_key() 的公共入口点，所有受影响的版本都缺少这些守卫。没有其他代码路径导致相同的泛型崩溃。负例检查 - 守卫完全是新增的，而非移除的逻辑。

fix_code_patterns: 函数入口处的显式参数 NULL 检查，并带有早期返回/错误处理
added_guards: ossl_ffc_validate_public_key_partial：(params == NULL || pub_key == NULL || params->p == NULL) → 返回 0 并设置 FFC_ERROR_PASSED_NULL_PARAM

root_cause_summary: Fencepost error in AES-NI OCB encrypt/decrypt grandloop for 32-bit x86. The $inp pointer points to the end of the already-processed 96-byte chunk. The loop condition uses `jb` (jump below, i.e., $inp < $len) instead of `jbe` (jump below or equal, i.e., $inp <= $len). When $inp == $len, exactly one full 96-byte chunk remains, but the strict-less-than comparison causes the loop to fall through to the 'short' tail, skipping the last 16-byte block of that chunk. This leaves 16 bytes of output memory unwritten (uninitialized data exposure) and produces an incorrect authentication tag.
fix_summary: Changed `jb` to `jbe` in two locations in crypto/aes/asm/aesni-x86.pl (one in aesni_ocb_encrypt grandloop exit, one in aesni_ocb_decrypt grandloop exit), correcting the loop continuation condition from strict less-than to less-than-or-equal, so that the final 96-byte chunk is fully processed when $inp == $len.

source_refs.snippet: The jump at the end of 'grandloop' had a fencepost error, doing a `jb` ('jump below') rather than `jbe` (jump below or equal).

necessary_conditions: 32-bit x86 platform using AES-NI OCB assembly implementation
Loop comparison uses strict less-than (jb) instead of less-than-or-equal (jbe)
Input length divisible by 96 (6 blocks) such that $inp == $len after a grandloop iteration
Assembly uses $inp as end-of-chunk pointer, not remaining-length counter
vulnerable_code_patterns: Assembly pattern: CMP <ptr>,<limit> followed by JB <loop> where JBE is required for correct boundary handling
OCB grandloop exit condition: &cmp ($inp,$len); &jb (&label("grandloop"))
negative_evidence: The 64-bit x86_64 AES-NI OCB implementation (aesni-x86_64.pl) is not affected — it uses a different loop structure
TLS/DTLS cipher suites using OCB are not affected — OpenSSL does not support OCB-based TLS/DTLS cipher suites
The C does not apply when the input length does not cause $inp to exactly equal $len at a grandloop boundary

fix_code_patterns: Changed JB to JBE in grandloop exit: &cmp ($inp,$len); &jbe (&label("grandloop"))

removed_vulnerable_logic: Removed the strict-less-than loop termination &jb (&label("grandloop")) at two sites
FixEvidence.semantic_change: Loop continuation condition widened from $inp < $len to $inp <= $len, so that when the input pointer exactly equals the end-of-96-byte-chunk pointer, the loop processes that final chunk instead of falling through and skipping the last 16-byte block.

root_cause_summary: ossl_policy_cache_set_mapping() at bad_mapping: attempts CRYPTO_THREAD_write_lock(x->lock) while x->lock is already held by the caller ossl_policy_cache_set()/policy_cache_new(). On non-recursive locks (Windows SRWLOCK, default PTHREAD_MUTEX) this causes a deadlock (CWE-667). The double-lock was introduced by commit 9aa4be69 which added lock/unlock around the EXFLAG_INVALID_POLICY flag set to fix a Coverity data-race warning, without noticing the caller already holds the lock.
fix_summary: Remove the entire CRYPTO_THREAD_write_lock / CRYPTO_THREAD_unlock block at the bad_mapping: label. The flag x->ex_flags |= EXFLAG_INVALID_POLICY is redundant because the caller's bad_cache: path in policy_cache_new() already performs this same flag set when ossl_policy_cache_set_mapping returns <= 0.

source_refs.snippet: x509: fix double locking problem. This reverts commit 9aa4be69 and removed the redundant flag setting.

root_cause_summary: xhci_ring_fetch and xhci_ring_chain_length in hw/usb/hcd-xhci.c each contain an unbounded while(1) loop that follows TR_LINK TRBs by updating the ring dequeue address. A malicious guest can construct an endless chain of link TRBs, causing QEMU to spin indefinitely (CWE-835 infinite loop / denial of service). No iteration limit existed on the number of consecutive link TRBs the emulator would chase.
fix_summary: Added #define TRB_LINK_LIMIT 4 and a uint32_t link_cnt counter initialized to 0 in both xhci_ring_fetch and xhci_ring_chain_length. Before following each TR_LINK, the counter is pre-incremented; if it exceeds TRB_LINK_LIMIT, the function returns early (0 in xhci_ring_fetch, -length in xhci_ring_chain_length), breaking the infinite loop.

root_cause_summary: Five independent memory leaks in QEMU vhost-user-gpu device (contrib/vhost-user-gpu/virgl.c and vhost-user-gpu.c) where allocated iovec arrays (res->iov, res_iovs) and buffer objects (res->buffer) were not freed on error paths, during resource destruction, or before being overwritten by a second attach_backing call. Four leak patterns: (1) virgl_cmd_resource_unref: resource unreferenced without detaching/freeing attached iovs; (2) virgl_resource_attach_backing: res_iovs not freed on attach_iov failure; (3) vg_resource_create_2d: res->buffer not destroyed on creation failure; (4) vg_resource_destroy: res->iov not freed; (5) vg_resource_attach_backing: no guard against double-attach causing old res->iov to leak.
fix_summary: Five add-only or mixed patches adding missing deallocation calls and early-return guards. (1) virgl_cmd_resource_unref: add virgl_renderer_resource_detach_iov + g_free before unref. (2) virgl_resource_attach_backing: capture attach_iov return, g_free(res_iovs) on failure. (3) vg_resource_create_2d: add vugbm_buffer_destroy before g_free(res) on error. (4) vg_resource_destroy: add g_free(res->iov). (5) vg_resource_attach_backing: add if (res->iov) early-return guard.

root_cause_summary: NIC emulators in loopback mode call nc->info->receive() directly, bypassing the reentrancy checks in qemu_net_queue_send() (queue->delivering flag) and the can_receive() precondition. When the receive callback re-enters the transmit path (e.g., via DMA completion or register write), this causes unbounded recursive calls resulting in stack overflow / CPU exhaustion (CWE-835: Loop with Unreachable Exit Condition).
fix_summary: Commit 705df5466c98 introduces qemu_receive_packet() which (1) checks qemu_can_receive_packet() (receive_disabled + can_receive) and (2) calls qemu_net_queue_receive() which checks queue->delivering to drop reentrant packets. Commit 705df5466c98 also refactors qemu_can_send_packet() to delegate to qemu_can_receive_packet(). The remaining 9 commits convert each NIC's loopback path from direct nc->info->receive() (or NIC-specific receive wrapper) to qemu_receive_packet() (or qemu_receive_packet_iov()).

int qemu_can_receive_packet(NetClientState *nc) { if (nc->receive_disabled) return 0; else if (nc->info->can_receive && !nc->info->can_receive(nc)) return 0; return 1; }
ssize_t qemu_receive_packet(NetClientState *nc, const uint8_t *buf, int size) { if (!qemu_can_receive_packet(nc)) return 0; return qemu_net_queue_receive(nc->incoming_queue, buf, size); }
ssize_t qemu_net_queue_receive(NetQueue *queue, const uint8_t *data, size_t size) { if (queue->delivering) return 0; return qemu_net_queue_deliver(queue, NULL, 0, data, size); }

root_cause_summary: Uncontrolled recursion in epan/dissectors/packet-bacapp.c function fAbstractSyntaxNType. The function is called from 15+ sites (lines 8655, 9305, 9521, 9690, 9700, 10117, 10377, 10884, 11484, 11564, 12110, 12913, 13219, 13838 in parent commit 8511253ab74280cb736e6ae1e6a0885dc9dbdca1). Sub-dissector functions like fPropertyValue, fActionList, fSimpleArrayComponent, fContextTaggedValue call back into fAbstractSyntaxNType, creating a mutual recursion chain. The parent function had no depth guard, so a crafted BACnet packet with deeply nested tags/constructs causes stack overflow -> crash (DoS). CWE-674 (primary): uncontrolled recursion leads to excessive stack consumption. CWE-74 (secondary): improper sanitization of protocol input enables the recursion trigger.
fix_summary: Added per-packet recursion depth tracking in fAbstractSyntaxNType using p_get_proto_data/p_add_proto_data (epan/proto_data.h). At function entry, depth is read from proto_data, incremented, and checked against BACAPP_MAX_RECURSION_DEPTH (100). If exceeded, an expert info warning (ei_bacapp_max_recursion_depth_reached) is emitted and the function returns early. At function exit, depth is decremented and written back. Depth is initialized to 0 in the top-level dissect_bacapp function. Also added the #define constant, expert field declaration, and expert field registration in proto_register_bacapp.

root_cause_summary: Three `for (;;)` unbounded loops in dissect_rfc7468() call tvb_find_line_end() with no progress guarantee. When offset is at/past tvb end and reassembly is off, tvb_find_line_end() returns linelen=0 (not -1) without advancing next_offset, so offset never changes and the loop spins infinitely, causing denial of service (CWE-835).
fix_summary: Replace each `for (;;)` with `while (tvb_offset_exists(tvb, offset))`, adding a bounds check on the tvb offset before every iteration. This exits the loop when offset exceeds tvb length instead of hanging forever.

source_refs.snippet: for (;;) → while (tvb_offset_exists(tvb, offset)) at loop 1 (preeb text lines)
for (;;) → while (tvb_offset_exists(tvb, offset)) at loop 2 (skip blank lines)
for (;;) → while (tvb_offset_exists(tvb, offset)) at loop 3 (base64 data)

If you're past the end of the tvbuff, tvb_find_line_end() will... return a zero-length line without advancing next_offset, so, unless you check for being past the end of the tvbuff, you'll loop forever.

necessary_conditions: Packet's tvbuff ends at or before current offset when entering one of the three processing loops
tvb_find_line_end() returns linelen=0 instead of -1 when offset is past tvb end with reassembly off
next_offset is not advanced (remains equal to offset) after the zero-length return
offset = next_offset assignment does not change offset, causing infinite loop
vulnerable_code_patterns: for (;;) { linelen = tvb_find_line_end(tvb, offset, -1, &next_offset, FALSE); ... offset = next_offset; }

fix_code_patterns: while (tvb_offset_exists(tvb, offset)) { linelen = tvb_find_line_end(tvb, offset, -1, &next_offset, FALSE); ... offset = next_offset; }
added_guards: Loop condition `tvb_offset_exists(tvb, offset)` — exits before body when offset exceeds tvb length
removed_vulnerable_logic: Unconditional `for (;;)` infinite-loop idiom replaced with bounded loop
FixEvidence.semantic_change: Loop entry is now guarded by a tvb bounds check. If offset is beyond tvb data, loop body never executes, preventing infinite spin.

preconditions: tvb_offset_exists(tvb, offset) must be true before each iteration
version_or_feature_guards: Vulnerable behavior depends on tvb_find_line_end() with reassembly=FALSE (no reassembly mode)

root_cause_summary: In epan/dissectors/packet-gquic.c:dissect_gquic_tag(), the function accumulates total_tag_len += tag_len across a while(tag_number) loop without checking for guint32 integer overflow. A crafted sequence of tags causes total_tag_len to wrap to a small value, so return offset + total_tag_len regresses the offset, leading to re-parsing the same data in the caller and an infinite loop (CWE-835). Additionally, the default: switch case continues parsing unknown tags via tag_offset += tag_len; with no early termination guard, allowing a malicious tag to sustain the loop.
fix_summary: The fix adds (1) a goto end; in the default: case to stop parsing after an unknown tag, (2) an end: label with integer overflow check if (offset + total_tag_len <= offset) that returns offset + tvb_reported_length_remaining(tvb, offset) to skip the remainder, and (3) registration of two new expert fields (ei_gquic_length_invalid, ei_gquic_invalid_parameter) for malformed packet reporting.

CertificatePolicy.reason: The fix directly addresses two co-dependent issues: (1) integer overflow in total_tag_len accumulation leading to offset regression, and (2) lack of early loop exit on unknown tags in the default: switch case. Both must be present for the vulnerability to be exploitable. The key verifiable certificate is the presence/absence of the overflow guard if (offset + total_tag_len <= offset) at the end: label and the goto end; replacement in the default: case. The expert field registrations are supporting infrastructure only and cannot serve as hard certificates independently.
